Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Tagliarini for Arndt Peltner (Executor).

The Court noted that it is missing verification of the cash balance and questions whether the extraordinary fees are reasonable. Mr. Tagliarini stated that he has the verification with him, that he tried to file it, that was told by the Clerk that it needs to be e-filed and that he will e-file it today. Mr. Tagliarini also stated that he tried to get the assets distributed to the Beneficiaries for a long time, that he was met with vigorous opposition, that things were appealed, that they finally reached a settlement and that he was here in Court 50 to 60 times over the course of the case. The Petition for Approval of Fourth and Final Accounting, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

The Court noted it does not have a problem with fees from both firms, but the time records do not add up to $182,000, inquired if there was $5,000 worth of time for the retainer paid upfront, and stated he can take the matters under submission or continue to allow counsel opportunity to file supplement. Mr. Tagliarini stated he would like the matter resolved to move forward with 4th accounting in January.

MINI MINUTES:

Steven N. H. Wood for Arndt Peltner;
Thomas C. Tagliarini for Arndt Peltner;
Carleton L. Briggs for and with Norman C. Herterich.

Matter called, the Court addresses the 2017 Civil Code of Procedure.

At the conclusion of the Court's comments, argument is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling with the exception of the following: (1) the submission and in camera review of the document identified as: discussions re: undated handwritten correspondence, and (2) as to issue of attorney client privilege.

The issues with regard to (1) the submission and in camera review of the document identified as: discussions re: undated handwritten correspondence, and (2) the issue of attorney client privilege are taken under submission.

The Court set the following supplemental briefing schedule on attorney client privilege and Evidence Codes 952 and 957:
Any additional briefing by Mr. Wood is due: November 1, 2018.
Any reply by Mr. Briggs is due: November 6, 2018.

The Court will review the supplemental briefing and document. If any additional argument is requested the Court will notify counsel.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Tagliarini for and with Arndt Peltner. Norman Herterich (Objector) in Propria Persona. Toufan Razi (Buyer) also present.

The Court stated that it didn't see anyway of moving forward until it had the Supreme Court decision. Mr. O'Sullivan stated that the issue has been that each beneficiary has not retained independent counsel and given the reluctance of the beneficiaries to retain separate counsel he didn't see anyway around it. Counsel stated that discovery still needed to be conducted, presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the scope of the appeal, the scope of the stay and stated that there was a disagreement as to the scope of the order with regard to the litigation Attorney's fees. The Court suggested Counsel continue talking, figure out what needs to be learned and continue mediation while the matter is on appeal. Mr. Tagliarini will outline what kind of discovery he will need by January 8, 2010. The matter was continued to March 23, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and for status of the appeal.

Judge Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For An Order Approving Interim Payment Of Attorney'S Fees And Expenses
Off Calendar Oct-22-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ciarin O'Sullivan for Norman Herterich. Thomas Tagliarini for Arndt Peltner (Petitioner).

Mr. Tagliarini stated that verification of the petition had been taken care of, that the Executor was present, that the Executor submitted their own declaration and presented a copy for the Court's review. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Petitioner being entitled to payment for legal fees, impartiality, possible conflict and appointment of a Special Administrator. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition For An Order Approving Interim Payment Of Attorney'S Fees And Expenses
Off Calendar Sep-28-2009 Continued To Oct-20-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine Distribution Rights
Off Calendar Sep-28-2009 Continued To Dec-22-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan for Norman Herterich. Alice Brown Traeg and Thomas Tagliarini for Arndt Petner.

Mr. O'Sullivan stated that some discovery still needed to be conducted. Ms. Traeg stated that the Inventory and Appraisals had been filed, but that one needed to be corrected. The matter was continued to December 22, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The pending Petition For Attorney's Fees on calendar for September 29, 2009 was continued to October 20, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Maria Lertore, CSR#11935. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

Petition To Determine Distribution Rights
Off Calendar Jun-29-2009 Continued To Sep-28-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan for and with Norman Herterich (Petitioner). Alice Brown Traeg and Thomas Tagliarini for Arndt Peltner (Executor).

Mr. O'Sullivan requested a 90 day continuance stating that they were in the middle of discovery, that he planned to conduct more discovery and that he would know if the matter would be ready for trial at that point. Mr. Tagliarini stated that a 90 day continuance was reasonable. The matter was continued to September 28, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on discovery.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Sum.

Petition To Determine Distribution Rights
Off Calendar Jun-08-2009 Continued To Jun-29-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
